summary: American minimalist composer (born 1937), the most-performed living American composer. Known for repetitive arpeggiated structures and additive rhythmic processes. Student of Nadia Boulanger. Created the Philip Glass Ensemble. Major works include the Portrait Trilogy operas (Einstein on the Beach, Satyagraha, Akhnaten), the film score Koyaanisqatsi, the symphonies Low and Heroes (after David Bowie albums), and the album Glassworks.

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: American minimalist composer, widely considered the most-performed living American composer. Student of Nadia Boulanger and Vincent Persichetti (who also taught him at Juilliard). Juilliard classmate of Steve Reich. Leads the Philip Glass Ensemble, for which he composed Music in Twelve Parts. Worked closely with the Mabou Mines theater company. Uses a characteristic ensemble of four saxophones, two flutes, three electric organs, and female voice. The play Philip Glass Buys a Loaf of Bread by David Ives parodies his minimalist style with lines like 'Go! Go! Go! Time! Time! Time!' Wikipedia

works.name: Einstein on the Beach
works.description: Five-hour opera with no conventional plot, created in collaboration with director Robert Wilson. First in Glass's Portrait Trilogy. Divided into three acts — 'Train,' 'Trial,' and 'Field/Spaceship' — separated by five 'Knee Plays' (intermezzos in which solfege syllables are chanted and numbers counted). A solo violinist is dressed as the title figure (Einstein). The audience is welcome to leave and return at any time. Includes text by Christopher Knowles ('Prematurely Air-Conditioned Supermarket' by Lucinda Childs, read from a bed) and choreography by Lucinda Childs. A bus driver narrates a story of love at the end of one Knee Play. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Five-hour opera with no plot; audience may come and go freely; first in the Portrait Trilogy; collaboration with Robert Wilson
examples: this five-hour opera without a plot, which has 'knee plays' occurring between its acts, which include 'Train' and 'Trial'

works.name: Satyagraha
works.description: Second opera in the Portrait Trilogy, about Mahatma Gandhi's early years in South Africa. Sung in Sanskrit. Third act contains the 'Newcastle March.' Miss Schlesen and Kallenbach sing a duet describing 'how the saints attained success.' Wikipedia

works.name: Akhnaten
works.description: Third opera in the Portrait Trilogy, about the monotheist pharaoh Akhenaten. Title role is a countertenor (who thus sings higher than his wife Nefertiti, a contralto). Celesta doubles the synthesizer; notably lacks violins, giving strings a stronger tone. An offstage chorus sings Psalm 104. The Hymn to the Sun ('Thou dost appear beautiful') in Act II is always performed in the audience's native language. A tour guide declares 'there is nothing left' of Akhenaten's temples in Act III. Phelim McDermott's staging uses juggling background performers. Title character is frequently doubled by the trumpet. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Third opera in the Portrait Trilogy; about the pharaoh Akhenaten; title role is a countertenor
examples: name this minimalist opera from the Portrait Trilogy, in which an offstage chorus sings Psalm 104
Akhnaten is an opera by this American composer, who grouped it with Satyagraha and Einstein on the Beach in his 'Portrait Trilogy'

clues.clue: Offstage chorus sings Psalm 104; opening line is 'Thou dost appear beautiful' (Hymn to the Sun); the Hymn to the Sun is always performed in the audience's native language
examples: the countertenor lead sings a second-act hymn beginning 'Thou dost appear beautiful,' and an offstage chorus quotes Psalm 104
Though Akhnaten features arias with libretti in Hebrew, Akkadian, and ancient Egyptian, this hymn from Act II of the opera is always performed in the audience's native language

works.name: Koyaanisqatsi (Film Score)
works.description: Score for Godfrey Reggio's 1982 experimental film. The title is a Hopi word meaning 'life out of balance.' Bass vocalists chant the five-syllable title repeatedly in an ominous descending four-note organ ostinato. The track 'Pruit Igoe' plays during the destruction of the Pruitt–Igoe housing complex. Part of a trilogy of Reggio films (followed by Powaqqatsi and Naqoyqatsi). Wikipedia

examples: Glass composed the score for this experimental film, in which the track 'Pruit Igoe' is played during the destruction of the namesake housing complex

works.name: Low Symphony and Heroes Symphony
works.description: Two symphonies based on David Bowie's Berlin Trilogy albums: Low (1992) and Heroes (1996). A third symphony based on Bowie's Lodger (Symphony No. 12) premiered in 2019. The Low Symphony's first movement is titled 'Subterraneans.' Fourth symphony has six movements and derives material from a song containing 'I wish you could swim / like the dolphins.' Wikipedia

clues.clue: Two symphonies based on David Bowie albums Low and Heroes; a third based on Lodger (Symphony No. 12) premiered in 2019
examples: This man wrote 'Low' and 'Heroes' symphonies based on David Bowie albums
Three of this composer's symphonies are based on the albums that form David Bowie's Berlin Trilogy

works.name: String Quartets
works.description: Nine string quartets in total. No. 2 ('Company') was incidental music for a Mabou Mines production of Beckett's novella Company; Glass describes it as a 'prose poem.' No. 3 ('Mishima') was extracted from the film score to Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters (performed by Kronos Quartet). No. 4 was written in memory of the artist Brian Buczak, commissioned by Geoffrey Hendricks, premiered at the Hauser Gallery. No. 5 contains movements 'Body Building' and 'Blood Oath.' A string sextet is a reduction of Glass's Third Symphony. Most recent quartet (No. 9, 2022) was inspired by King Lear. The Kronos Quartet recorded many of these works. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Nine string quartets total; No. 2 ('Company') based on Beckett's Company; No. 3 ('Mishima') extracted from the Mishima film score; Kronos Quartet recorded them

This composer's string sextet is a reduction of his strings-only Third Symphony

works.name: Glassworks
works.description: 1982 album of six-movement chamber music, intended to popularize Glass's work. Includes the movements 'Floe,' 'Island,' 'Rubric,' and 'Facades.' Glass created a special 'Sony Walkman mix' for the album. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Six-movement chamber album including 'Floe,' 'Island,' 'Rubric,' and 'Facades'; Glass created a 'Sony Walkman mix' for it

This composer attempted to popularize his work with a six-movement chamber composition including movements titled 'Floe,' 'Island,' and 'Rubric'

works.name: Other Works
works.description: Other notable works by Philip Glass clued in quizbowl. 1000 Airplanes on the Roof: one-person opera (collaboration with David Henry Hwang) about a man who claims to have met aliens; largely a monologue by a character 'M' on a holographic set. The Perfect American: opera depicting Walt Disney on his deathbed, discussing the cryogenic preservation conspiracy. The Trial: 2014 opera based on Kafka's unfinished novel, libretto by Christopher Hampton. Songs from Liquid Days: album with lyrics by Suzanne Vega (movement 'Freezing') and Paul Simon. Metamorphosis: five piano pieces; Pearl Jam typically opens concerts with 'Metamorphosis Two.' Mad Rush: piano piece inspired by the Dalai Lama's visit to New York. 20 Piano Etudes: completed 2014. Symphony No. 7 (Toltec): Mesoamerican-inspired; uses wood-blocks and glockenspiels. Concerto Fantasy for Two Timpanists: timpanists must play with their hands for a higher pitch.

comprehensive_summary:
Philip Glass (born 1937) is an American minimalist composer widely recognized as the most-performed living American composer. He studied with Nadia Boulanger in Paris and with Vincent Persichetti at Juilliard, where he was a classmate of Steve Reich. He leads the Philip Glass Ensemble, for which he composed Music in Twelve Parts — a three-hour work originally misunderstood to have twelve movements when Glass actually meant twelve musical lines. Glass has long association with the Mabou Mines theater company (for whom he wrote Music for Voices) and collaborated extensively with the Kronos Quartet, which recorded his string quartets and various film scores.

Glass's most celebrated achievement is his 'Portrait Trilogy' of operas about three world-historical figures: Einstein on the Beach (1976, Albert Einstein), Satyagraha (1979, Gandhi), and Akhnaten (1983, the Egyptian pharaoh Akhenaten). Einstein on the Beach, created in collaboration with director Robert Wilson, is a five-hour opera with no conventional plot. Audiences are welcome to leave and return at will. It is divided into three acts — 'Train,' 'Trial,' and 'Field/Spaceship' — separated by five 'Knee Plays' in which chorus members count numbers and chant solfege syllables. A solo violinist is dressed as Einstein throughout. Text contributions came from Christopher Knowles and Lucinda Childs, including the section 'Prematurely Air-Conditioned Supermarket' read by Childs from a bed. The opera opens with a white square highlighting two figures seated at desks. A Los Angeles Opera production staged dancers in suspenders on scaffolding in front of LED circles. Satyagraha is sung entirely in Sanskrit, and its third act contains the 'Newcastle March'; Miss Schlesen and Kallenbach sing a duet about 'how the saints attained success.' Akhnaten features a countertenor in the title role, meaning Akhenaten sings higher notes than his contralto wife Nefertiti. The opera lacks violins (the celesta doubles the synthesizer), its offstage chorus sings Psalm 104, and the 'Hymn to the Sun' ('Thou dost appear beautiful') from Act II is always performed in the audience's native language. The libretto uses Hebrew, Akkadian, and ancient Egyptian. A tour guide in Act III declares 'there is nothing left' of Akhenaten's temples. Phelim McDermott's celebrated staging uses juggling performers in the background.

Glass's film scores are equally prominent in quizbowl. Koyaanisqatsi (1982), directed by Godfrey Reggio, opens with an ominous descending four-note organ ostinato while bass vocalists repeatedly chant the five-syllable Hopi title ('life out of balance'). The track 'Pruit Igoe' accompanies the demolition of the Pruitt–Igoe housing complex. It is the first in a Reggio trilogy. Glass also scored Martin Scorsese's Kundun, received an Academy Award nomination for The Hours, and collaborated with Errol Morris on A Brief History of Time and The Thin Blue Line.

Glass composed two celebrated symphonies based on David Bowie's Berlin Trilogy albums: the Low Symphony and the Heroes Symphony. Symphony No. 12, based on Bowie's Lodger, premiered in 2019. Glass has composed nine string quartets; No. 2 ('Company') was incidental music for a Mabou Mines production of Samuel Beckett's Company; Glass calls the novella a 'prose poem.' No. 3 ('Mishima') was extracted from his Mishima: A Life in Four Chapters soundtrack. No. 4 was commissioned by Geoffrey Hendricks, premiered at the Hauser Gallery, and was written in memory of the artist Brian Buczak. The most recent (No. 9, 2022) was inspired by King Lear. The Kronos Quartet has recorded many of them.

Other important works: Glassworks (1982) is a six-movement album including 'Floe,' 'Island,' 'Rubric,' and 'Facades,' with a special 'Sony Walkman mix'; Songs from Liquid Days set lyrics by Suzanne Vega (in the movement 'Freezing') accompanied by a string quartet; the piano album Solo Piano includes five 'Metamorphosis' pieces (Pearl Jam traditionally opens concerts with 'Metamorphosis Two') and Mad Rush (inspired by the Dalai Lama's visit to New York); 1000 Airplanes on the Roof is a one-person opera about alien abduction, created with playwright David Henry Hwang; The Perfect American is an opera about Walt Disney on his deathbed; The Trial (2014) adapted Kafka with a libretto by Christopher Hampton; and the Concerto Fantasy for Two Timpanists requires the players to use their hands for higher pitches. The play Philip Glass Buys a Loaf of Bread by David Ives (in the anthology All in the Timing) parodies Glass's style with lines like 'Go! Go! Go! Time! Time! Time!'

cards.front: Composer: American minimalist, most-performed living American composer; leads the Philip Glass Ensemble; studied with Nadia Boulanger and Vincent Persichetti
Composer: composed Music in Twelve Parts for his ensemble — the title refers to 12 musical lines, not movements, after a friend's misunderstanding
Composer: the David Ives play Philip Glass Buys a Loaf of Bread (in All in the Timing) parodies his minimalist style with lines like 'Go! Go! Go! Time! Time! Time!'
Composer: nominated for an Academy Award for The Hours; also scored Kundun (Scorsese) and collaborated with Errol Morris on A Brief History of Time and The Thin Blue Line
Opera: five-hour work with no plot; audience may come and go freely; created with Robert Wilson; first in the Portrait Trilogy
Opera: divided into acts 'Train,' 'Trial,' and 'Field/Spaceship' separated by five 'Knee Plays' in which chorus members count numbers and chant solfege syllables
Opera: a solo violinist is dressed as Einstein throughout; section 'Prematurely Air-Conditioned Supermarket' uses text by Lucinda Childs read from a bed
Opera: about Gandhi; sung entirely in Sanskrit; third act contains the 'Newcastle March'; second in the Portrait Trilogy
Opera: about the pharaoh Akhenaten; title role is a countertenor (sings higher than his contralto wife Nefertiti); offstage chorus sings Psalm 104; lacks violins; third in the Portrait Trilogy
Opera: Act II 'Hymn to the Sun' ('Thou dost appear beautiful') is always performed in the audience's native language; libretto uses Hebrew, Akkadian, and ancient Egyptian
Opera: Phelim McDermott's staging uses juggling background performers; Act III features a tour guide declaring 'there is nothing left' of the title figure's temples

Film score: Godfrey Reggio film; title means 'life out of balance' in Hopi; bass vocalists chant the five-syllable title over an ominous descending organ ostinato; 'Pruit Igoe' track plays during the demolition of the Pruitt–Igoe housing complex
Symphonies: Low Symphony and Heroes Symphony are based on David Bowie's Berlin Trilogy albums; Symphony No. 12 (based on Lodger) premiered in 2019
String Quartet: No. 2 ('Company') based on Beckett's novella Company; No. 3 ('Mishima') extracted from Mishima film score; nine total; Kronos Quartet recorded them
Album: six-movement chamber work including 'Floe,' 'Island,' 'Rubric,' and 'Facades'; Glass created a special 'Sony Walkman mix' for it
Opera: one-person work about a man named 'M' who claims to have met aliens; set on a holographic stage; created with David Henry Hwang
Composer: Pearl Jam typically opens concerts with his piano piece 'Metamorphosis Two'; Mad Rush was inspired by the Dalai Lama's visit to New York
